sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Key findings from the diff:
1. The red block moved from rows 30-33, cols 12-17 to rows 24-29, cols 18-21
2. The white outline around the red target disappeared (W pixels decreased from 182 to 101, and the white outline object at rows 33-39, cols 41-55 was removed)
3. The background colors shifted slightly (likely due to the red block moving)
So clicking on the red target on the right side caused the red block on the left to move to a new position! The red block moved from its original position to a position near the blue block.
This suggests the game mechanic is:
- Clicking on a target shape on the right side causes the corresponding colored block on the left to move
- The goal might be to position all blocks correctly
Let me look at the current state more carefully. The red block is now at rows 24-29, cols 18-21. The blue block is at rows 20-23, cols 18-21. So the red block is now directly below the blue block.
Looking at the image, I can see:
- The red target on the right no longer has a white outline
- The left side now shows the red block moved up next to the blue block
- The blue target on the right still has its white outline
So the mechanic seems to be:
- Clicking on a target removes its white outline and moves the corresponding colored block
- The goal is probably to click on all targets to "complete" them
Let me try clicking on the blue target to see if it does the same thing.

sections.content:

<parameter=code>

action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 36, 'col': 48}])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
